description: Static resolution pass for the 23 GAME.OVR source-recovery role hypotheses.
limits: Resolved means removed from the unknown queue by static evidence.
Resolved does not mean runtime-validated or original-name recovery.
Stub/boundary and bad-decode resolutions are intentionally not liftable into runtime behavior.

resolutions.summary: Calls a shared initializer, sets global flag 0x4417, and copies global mode/state byte 0x3ddb into 0x50d2.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use as evidence for overlay/global state initialization, not as a credits routine.
resolutions.next_validation: Find callers around overlay entry and compare 0x4417/0x50d2 meanings against runtime state transitions.
resolutions.name: FUN_0000_016c

resolutions.summary: Ghidra reports overlapping instructions, bad instruction data, abnormal stack setup, and removed blocks.
resolutions.implementation_use: Do not lift this pseudocode; treat the address as a bad entry or mixed code/data island.
resolutions.next_validation: Inspect raw bytes and Ghidra disassembly manually before assigning any behavior.
resolutions.name: FUN_0000_0385

resolutions.summary: Initializes combat tables, walks the party linked list, records combatant pointers, status/facing, visibility, and battlefield placement entries.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use to validate combat start roster construction and initial placement logic.
resolutions.next_validation: Compare combatant table counts and positions against a DOS combat capture.
resolutions.name: FUN_0000_fb92

resolutions.summary: Decompiles to a tiny return-only function near the combat_start anchor.
resolutions.implementation_use: Do not lift as combat logic; keep as possible boundary, padding, or misidentified function start.
resolutions.next_validation: Check neighboring raw bytes and cross-references before deleting from the combat cluster.
resolutions.name: FUN_1000_0131

resolutions.summary: Loops 0x55 bytes, calls a byte-producing helper, and writes into the active character record at offset +0x4d onward.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use to validate character-creation default/stat/equipment field initialization.
resolutions.next_validation: Compare generated character records after DOS character creation with reconstructed defaults.
resolutions.name: FUN_1000_8467

resolutions.summary: If a roster/entry marker equals 7, removes an entry and compacts subsequent character indices while decrementing the active count.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use to validate character removal or rejected-created-character roster compaction.
resolutions.next_validation: Capture DOS behavior for deleting/dropping a created character and compare roster order.
resolutions.name: FUN_1000_8e7d

resolutions.summary: Builds item records from table bytes, links them into the active character inventory list, and marks an item flag bit.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use to validate shop purchase/reward item insertion.
resolutions.next_validation: Compare inventory linked-list changes after buying known shop items.
resolutions.name: FUN_1000_c010

resolutions.summary: Decompiles to a tiny return-only function near the shop_menu anchor.
resolutions.implementation_use: Do not lift as shop logic; likely boundary/padding or bad function start.
resolutions.next_validation: Inspect neighboring shop-menu code and xrefs.
resolutions.name: FUN_1000_ce56

resolutions.summary: Reads an item type byte from the loaded item table and dispatches item classes 1..6 through FUN_1000_cf61; otherwise advances the item pointer.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use to validate ITEM0 category mapping and item-detail decode.
resolutions.next_validation: Compare dispatch buckets against decoded ITEM0 names and item detail records.
resolutions.name: FUN_1000_eb60

resolutions.summary: Checks equipment/status flags at 0x8c33..0x8c3a, clears them, updates globals, and calls display/result helpers.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use as evidence for post-item/equipment state refresh, not base item loading.
resolutions.next_validation: Trigger equip/unequip/use flows in DOS and compare flags/UI side effects.
resolutions.name: FUN_1000_eeec

resolutions.summary: Uses large stack buffers, menu/input/file helper calls, and returns a success flag after validating selected save/character output.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use to validate save-character/save-slot prompting and file validation.
resolutions.next_validation: Compare DOS save prompts and invalid-slot behavior.
resolutions.name: FUN_1000_f67d

resolutions.summary: Calls the save selection helper, computes serialized linked-list sizes, copies party/character state, checks output size, and branches into write/error routines.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use to validate save serialization sizing and character-state fields.
resolutions.next_validation: Compare produced SAVGAMA/character-save byte lengths and selected offsets.
resolutions.name: FUN_1000_fad0

resolutions.summary: Walks a linked chain at character offsets 0xfb/0xfd in 9-byte increments and calls save/write helpers, including the save writer.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use to validate serialized item-chain layout.
resolutions.next_validation: Compare saved inventory/equipment item-chain bytes against DOS saves.
resolutions.name: FUN_1000_fb92

resolutions.summary: Walks party members and nested linked item/equipment chains to compute serialized save size using 0x103, 0x3e, and 9-byte record increments.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use to validate save sizing before save/load byte-format work.
resolutions.next_validation: Assert computed size against known DOS save samples.
resolutions.name: FUN_2000_13fd

resolutions.summary: Decompiles to a tiny return-only function near the save_game anchor.
resolutions.implementation_use: Do not lift as save logic; keep as boundary/padding candidate.
resolutions.next_validation: Inspect adjacent save-game bytes and xrefs.
resolutions.name: FUN_2000_1c53

resolutions.summary: Counts linked records from a character pointer at offsets 0xf7/0xf9 by following +0x2a/+0x2c next pointers.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use to validate character sheet inventory counts.
resolutions.next_validation: Compare count with visible character sheet inventory slots.
resolutions.name: FUN_2000_1fd4

resolutions.summary: Calls a display/setup helper and copies up to 0xff bytes into a local text/display buffer.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use as character-sheet display helper evidence only.
resolutions.next_validation: Trace caller context and compare emitted text in DOS character sheet.
resolutions.name: FUN_2000_25a1

resolutions.summary: Chooses display attribute 0x0d/0x0f, fetches item-related text/data through FUN_0000_733e, and writes a short display buffer.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use to validate inventory/gear list line rendering.
resolutions.next_validation: Compare inventory line highlighting/equipped markers in DOS.
resolutions.name: FUN_2000_2986

resolutions.summary: Walks a character item chain, filters equipped/flagged records, and returns the first item whose category table entry matches the requested category.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use to validate equipped item lookup by item category.
resolutions.next_validation: Compare weapon/armor selection effects against item category table.
resolutions.name: FUN_2000_3ea6

resolutions.summary: Checks whether a ship/vehicle record is valid, adds active/alternate modifiers, applies damage penalties, and clamps the result to at least 1.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use to validate ship movement/maneuver/status derived values.
resolutions.next_validation: Compare ship stats before and after hull/system damage in DOS.
resolutions.name: FUN_2000_b749

resolutions.summary: Sets global ship status flag 0x535b based on mode and ship record state, calls a UI helper, then emits a message/display block.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use to validate ship status UI and travel gating.
resolutions.next_validation: Compare status/message display for active ship versus alternate target.
resolutions.name: FUN_2000_b876

resolutions.summary: Runs repeated 4/3/2-count loops over byte-producing helpers and emit helpers, matching a span/segment decode pattern before the WALLDEF/Load3DMap string/data region.
resolutions.implementation_use: Use as candidate wall segment decoder/emitter. Raw disassembly resolves it as not the Load3DMap file-load/copy routine.
resolutions.next_validation: Trace helper xrefs and compare emitted sequence against WALLDEF rendering artifacts.
resolutions.name: FUN_3000_006f

resolutions.summary: Ghidra entry 3000:0c04 lands inside the instruction at payload 0x30C01 in the Load3DMap-shaped routine epilogue, so the tiny return decompile is a misaligned-entry artifact.
resolutions.implementation_use: Do not lift as a function. The resolved Load3DMap-shaped code window is payload 0x30AD0-0x30C10.
resolutions.next_validation: Use generated/source_recovery/load3dmap_cluster_resolution.md before making map-loader runtime changes.
resolutions.name: FUN_3000_0c04
